title: Baccaurea sumatrana
what_description: Baccaurea sumatrana is a species of plant in the family Phyllanthaceae. It is an evergreen tree that grows up to 20 meters tall, with a trunk diameter of up to 60 centimeters. The leaves are simple, alternate, and elliptical in shape, measuring 8-15 centimeters long and 3-6 centimeters wide, with a prominent midrib and 7-9 pairs of lateral veins. The inflorescence is a panicle or a spike, bearing small, greenish-yellow flowers with 5-7 petals, and the fruit is a drupe, 2-3 centimeters long and 1.5-2 centimeters wide, with a brown, leathery pericarp. Baccaurea sumatrana is native to Southeast Asia, specifically in countries such as Indonesia, Malaysia, and Thailand.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Baccaurea sumatrana are inflammation, which is alleviated by its anti-inflammatory properties, as well as diabetes, where it has been shown to lower blood glucose levels. Hypertension is also controlled by Baccaurea sumatrana, due to its ability to relax blood vessels. Additionally, cancer cells have been inhibited in studies, and asthma symptoms have been reduced by its bronchodilatory effects. Furthermore, rheumatism and gastritis have been alleviated by Baccaurea sumatrana, and it has been used to treat cardiovascular disease, infections, gastrointestinal issues, gastroenteritis, kidney stones, cholesterol, gout, gastroesophageal reflux disease, and provide pain relief.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Baccaurea sumatrana are its antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory activities, which have been attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and saponins. The plant's antioxidant properties can be attributed to its high levels of flavonoids, which have been shown to scavenge free radicals and protect against oxidative stress. Its antimicrobial properties are due to the presence of saponins, which have been demonstrated to inhibit the growth of various microorganisms. The anti-inflammatory properties of Baccaurea sumatrana have been linked to the action of its volatile oils, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. Additionally, the plant has been found to possess cardioprotective properties, likely due to the presence of bioactive compounds that help to protect against cardiovascular disease.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Baccaurea sumatrana are flavonoids, tannins, phenolic acids, saponins, and glycosides. Flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to the plant's ability to reduce inflammation and protect against oxidative stress. Tannins, such as gallic acid and ellagic acid, have been found to exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral activities, which may help to prevent infections and promote wound healing. Phenolic acids, including ferulic acid and sinapic acid, have been reported to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to protect against cell damage and promote tissue repair. The saponins and glycosides present in Baccaurea sumatrana may also contribute to its healing properties by exhibiting antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Baccaurea sumatrana are infusion, tea, tincture, decoction, and Baccaurea sumatrana tea, which are derived from its various parts, including the fruit, leaves, and bark. Infusion and decoction are liquid preparations made by steeping the plant material in hot water, while tincture is a concentrated liquid extract made by soaking the plant in a solvent, such as ethanol or glycerin. Tea, a more diluted form of infusion, is often consumed for its medicinal properties. Baccaurea sumatrana tea is a specific preparation made by infusing the fruit or leaves in hot water, which is traditionally consumed in Southeast Asia for its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The choice of preparation depends on the desired potency and solubility of the active compounds present in the plant.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Baccaurea sumatrana improperly are numerous and can be severe. If ingested in excess or without proper preparation, Baccaurea sumatrana can cause skin irritation due to the presence of allergenic compounds and sapogenins, which can trigger allergic reactions and irritation. Ingestion of large quantities can also lead to gastrointestinal issues such as diarrhea and nausea due to the presence of toxic saponins and alkaloids, which can disrupt the normal functioning of the digestive system. Furthermore, improper use of Baccaurea sumatrana can cause dizziness and lightheadedness due to the presence of psychoactive alkaloids, which can affect the central nervous system and impair cognitive function. In extreme cases, excessive consumption of Baccaurea sumatrana can lead to seizures due to the disruption of the normal functioning of the nervous system.

The fruit of the plant is rich in antioxidants and has been studied for its potential anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer properties. intro: Baccaurea sumatrana, also known as Sumatran Cupuacu, is a species of fruit-bearing tree native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its potential health benefits such as antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Baccaurea sumatrana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or a tincture, with the latter being used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
